Item 1.  Business
Introduction
Goldman Sachs is a leading global financial institution that delivers a broad range of financial services to a large and diversified client base that includes corporations, financial institutions, governments and individuals. Our purpose is to advance sustainable economic growth and financial opportunity. Our goal, reflected in our One Goldman Sachs initiative, is to deliver the full range of our services and expertise to support our clients in a more accessible, comprehensive and efficient manner, across businesses and product areas.
When we use the terms "Goldman Sachs," "we," "us," "our" and "the firm," we mean The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (Group Inc. or parent company), a Delaware corporation, and its consolidated subsidiaries. When we use the term "our subsidiaries," we mean the consolidated subsidiaries of Group Inc. References to "this Form 10-K" are to our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2023. All references to 2023, 2022 and 2021 refer to our years ended, or the dates, as the context requires, December 31, 2023, December 31, 2022 and December 31, 2021, respectively.
Group Inc. is a bank holding company (BHC) and a financial holding company (FHC) regulated by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (FRB). Our U.S. depository insti
…[truncated 20688 chars]…
regulatory requirements, including those of FINRA. Because we are a BHC, our Business Continuity & Technology Resilience Program is also subject to review by the FRB. The key elements of the program are crisis management, business continuity, technology resilience, business recovery, assurance and verification, and process improvement. In the area of information security, we have developed and implemented a framework of principles, policies and technology designed to protect the information provided to us by our clients and our own information from cyber attacks and other misappropriation, corruption or loss. Safeguards are designed to maintain the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information.
Human Capital Management
Our people are our greatest asset. We believe that a major strength and principal reason for our success is the quality, dedication, determination and collaboration of our people, which enables us to serve our clients, generate long-term value for our shareholders and contribute to the broader community. We invest heavily in developing and supporting our people throughout their careers, and we strive to maintain a work environment that fosters professionalism, excellence, high standards of business ethics, diversity, teamwork and cooperation among our employees worldwide.
Diversity and Inclusion
The strength of our culture, our ability to execute our strategy, and our relationships with clients all depend on a diverse workforce and an inclusive work environment that encourages a wide range of perspectives. We believe that diversity at all levels of our organization, from entry-level analysts to senior management, as well as the Board of Directors of Group Inc. (Board) is essential to our sustainability. As of December 2023, approximately 54% of our Board was diverse by race, gender or sexual orientation. Our management team works closely with our Global Inclusion and Diversity Committee to foster the diversity of our global workforce at all levels. In addition, we have Inclusion and Diversity Committees across regions, which promote an environment that values different perspectives, challenges conventional thinking and maximizes the potential of all our people.
We believe diversity, including diversity of experience, gender identity, race, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability and veteran status, in addition to being a social imperative, is vital to our commercial success through the creativity that it fosters. For this reason, we have established a comprehensive action plan with aspirational diversity hiring and representation goals which are set forth below and are focused on cultivating an inclusive environment for all our colleagues.
Goldman Sachs 2023 Form 10-K 5
THE GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP, INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES
Diverse leadership is crucial to our long-term success and to driving innovation, and we have implemented and expanded outreach and career development programs for rising diverse executive talent. For example, we are focused on ensuring that vice presidents, including diverse vice presidents, have the necessary coaching, sponsorship and advocacy to support their career trajectories and strengthen their leadership platforms. Many other career development initiatives are aimed at fostering talent, including diverse talent, at the analyst and associate level. Our global and regional Inclusion Networks and Interest Forums are open to all professionals at Goldman Sachs to promote and advance connectivity, understanding, inclusion and diversity.
Partner and Managing Director Promotions and Progress Toward Aspirational Goals
The composition of our most recent partnership class was 29% women professionals, 24% Asian professionals, 9% Black professionals, 3% Hispanic/Latinx professionals, 3% LGBTQ+ professionals and 3% professionals who are military/veterans. The composition of our most recent managing director class was 31% women professionals, 31% Asian professionals, 2% Black professionals, 4% Hispanic/Latinx professionals, 3% LGBTQ+ professionals and 3% professionals who are military/veterans.
We have also set forth the following aspirational goals:
- Analyst and associate hiring of 50% women professionals, 11% Black professionals and 14% Hispanic/Latinx professionals in the Americas, and 9% Black professionals in the U.K. In 2023, our analyst and associate hires included 49% women professionals, 9% Black professionals and 13% Hispanic/Latinx professionals in the Americas, and 15% Black professionals in the U.K.
- Women professionals to represent 40% of our vice presidents globally by 2025, and women professionals to comprise 50% of our employees globally over time. As of December 2023, women professionals represented 33% of our vice president population globally and women professionals represented 42% of our employees globally. In addition, women professionals constituted 32% of senior talent (vice presidents and above) in the U.K., above the 30% goal for U.K. senior talent (vice presidents and above).
- Black professionals to represent 7% of our vice president population in the Americas and in the U.K., and for Hispanic/Latinx professionals to represent 9% of our vice president population in the Americas, both by 2025. As of December 2023, Black professionals represented 4% of our vice president population in the Americas and 5% in the U.K., and Hispanic/Latinx professionals represented 7% of our vice president population in the Americas.
- Doubling the number of campus hires in the U.S. recruited from Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) in 2025 relative to 2020.
Other than title, the metrics above are based on self-identification.
Talent Development and Retention
We seek to help our people achieve their full potential by investing in them and supporting a culture of continuous development. Our goals are to maximize individual capabilities, increase commercial effectiveness and innovation, reinforce our culture, expand professional opportunities, and help our people contribute positively to their communities.
Instilling our culture in all employees is a continuous process, in which training plays an important part. We offer our employees the opportunity to participate in ongoing educational offerings and periodic seminars facilitated by our Learning & Engagement team. To accelerate their integration into the firm and our culture, new hires have the opportunity to receive training before they start working via orientation programs that emphasize culture and networking, and nearly all employees participate in at least one training event each year. For our more senior employees, we provide guidance and training on how to manage people and projects effectively, exhibit strong leadership and exemplify our culture. We are also focused on developing a high performing, diverse leadership pipeline and career planning for our next generation of leaders. We maintain a variety of programs aimed at employees' professional growth and leadership development, including initiatives, such as our Vice President and Managing Director Leadership Acceleration Initiatives and Partner Development Initiative.
Enhancing our people's experience of internal mobility is a key focus, as we believe that this will inspire employees, help retain top talent and create diverse experiences to build future leaders.
Another important part of instilling our culture is our employee performance review process. Employees are reviewed by supervisors, co-workers and employees whom they supervise in a 360-degree review process that is integral to our team approach and includes an evaluation of an employee's performance with respect to risk management, protecting our reputation, adherence to our code of conduct, compliance, and diversity and inclusion principles. Our approach to evaluating employee performance centers on providing robust, timely and actionable feedback that facilitates profess

GS.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Report: GS (Goldman Sachs Group)  
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**  

---

#### 1. **Financial Trajectory**  
- **Revenue**: TTM revenue (4Q 2023) is $46.25B. Quarterly revenue has been volatile: Q1 2023 was $12.22B, Q4 2023 was $11.32B (-4.2% QoQ, +6.8% YoY).  
- **Earnings**: TTM net income is $8.52B. Quarterly EPS diluted ranged from $3.08 (Q2 2023) to $10.76 (Q1 2022). Latest Q4 2023 EPS was $5.48 (+63.6% YoY).  
- **Margins**: Operating margin for Q4 2023 is 19.9% ($2.25B operating income / $11.32B revenue). Net margin for Q4 2023 is 17.8% ($2.01B net income / $11.32B revenue). Margins have fluctuated significantly (e.g., Q1 2023 operating margin: 32.6%).  
- **Cash Flow**: No cash flow data available for recent quarters.  
- **Balance Sheet**: Total equity stable around $116–$117B over 8 quarters (Q4 2023: $116.91B). Total assets grew from $1,441.80B (Q4 2022) to $1,641.59B (Q4 2023). Leverage (total assets / total equity) is 14.0x as of Q4 2023.  

---

#### 2. **Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings Quality**: High volatility in quarterly EPS (range $3.08–$10.76 over 8 quarters) suggests earnings are sensitive to market conditions. TTM net margin of 18.4% (per valuation ratios) aligns with recent quarterly net margins (17.8% in Q4 2023).  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity base stable ($116.91B in Q4 2023 vs. $117.19B in Q4 2022). However, high leverage (14.0x) and asset growth may indicate increased risk exposure.  

---

#### 3. **Valuation vs. History**  
- **P/E (diluted)**: 18.264 as of 2024-03-28, at the top of its 365-day range (10.4–18.3).  
- **P/B**: 1.236 as of 2024-03-28, above its 365-day range (0.9–1.2).  
- **ROE**: 7.3% as of 2024-03-28, down from 9.6% in April 2023.  
- **Net Margin**: 18.4% as of 2024-03-28, within its 365-day range (0.2–0.2).  
- **Earnings Yield**: 5.5% as of 2024-03-28.  

**Context**: Current P/E and P/B multiples are elevated relative to the past year, while ROE has declined.  

---

#### 4. **Price & Return Context**  
- **Price Level**: $417.69 on 2024-03-28, near the 52-week high of $419.20.  
- **Quarterly Return (Q1 2024)**: +8.6% (from $384.48 on 2023-12-27).  
- **Annual Return (1-Year)**: +31.2% (from $318.44 on 2023-03-28).  
- **Path**: Price rose from $289.36 (52-week low) to $417.69, outpacing the fundamental path (e.g., TTM earnings growth of 51.4% YoY in Q4 2023).  

---

#### 5. **Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**  
- **Upside**: YoY earnings growth of 63.6% in Q4 2023; stable equity base; if margins stabilize at ~18–19%, current P/E may be supported.  
- **Downside**: Elevated P/E (18.3) at top of range; declining ROE (from 9.6% to 7.3%); high leverage (14.0x); volatile quarterly earnings (e.g., Q2 2023 EPS of $3.08).  

---

#### 6. **Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
**Quantitative Risk/Reward**: The stock trades at a high P/E relative to its history, with declining ROE and volatile earnings. Price appreciation (+31.2% annual) has outpaced earnings growth, pushing valuation to the upper end of its range. Balance sheet leverage adds risk. Without cash flow data, cash generation cannot be assessed. Upside is limited unless earnings stabilize and ROE recovers; downside is elevated if earnings volatility persists or multiples revert to historical averages.
GS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: Goldman Sachs (GS)**
**Evidence Cutoff:** 2024-04-01 | **Date of Analysis:** 2024-04-01

### 1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K filed 2024-02-23)
Goldman Sachs is a leading global financial institution organized into three business segments:
*   **Global Banking & Markets:** The firm's traditional core, generating revenues from investment banking advisory/underwriting fees, FICC (Fixed Income, Currency and Commodities) and Equities intermediation/financing, and related lending/investing. GS is a major market maker across thousands of products.
*   **Asset & Wealth Management (AWM):** Provides investment and wealth management services across equity, fixed income, and alternative strategies for institutional and individual clients. Includes private banking/lending (via Marcus), equity/debt investments, and management/incentive fees. The firm is reducing historical principal investments.
*   **Platform Solutions:** Houses consumer platforms (credit card partnerships like GreenSky, Apple Card) and transaction banking (cash management, payments). GS is actively divesting parts of this segment (selling GreenSky, transitioning the GM card program).

The franchise is characterized by a diversified, global institutional client base and a strategy ("One Goldman Sachs") to deliver integrated services.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated, from Supplied Text)
*   **2024-03-21:** U.S. bank stocks hit all-time highs, fueled by expectations of stronger economic growth and reduced borrowing costs following the March FOMC meeting.
*   **2024-03-22:** GS shares declined more than the broader market (-1.67%). No specific reason provided in the summary.
*   **2024-02-27:** Edward Jones initiated coverage of GS with a "Buy" rating, highlighting its leading position in investment banking as a key factor for a positive outlook, anticipating gains from an improving capital markets environment.
*   **2024-02-19:** Goldman Sachs' own research raised its 2024 S&P 500 target to 5,200, citing improved U.S. economic outlook and stronger mega-cap profit margins.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Litigation & Legacy Risk (Ongoing):** The firm faces a lawsuit filed on **2024-03-17** (ACA Financial Guaranty) alleging fraud related to historical CDO transactions (ABACUS). This underscores persistent reputational and financial risks from pre-financial crisis activities.
*   **Regulatory Penalties (Recent):** On **2024-02-22**, Goldman Sachs received a disciplinary notice and penalty from ICE's Business Conduct Committee for alleged trading violations dating from July 2022, indicating ongoing regulatory scrutiny.
*   **Business Model Cyclicality:** The Global Banking & Markets segment, particularly investment banking and FICC, is inherently tied to capital market activity and macroeconomic conditions, which remain uncertain.

**Offsets:**
*   **Strong Market Position:** The firm's own description and external initiation (**2024-02-27**) cite a leadership position in investment banking, a key franchise strength.
*   **Favorable Macro Backdrop:** Recent market narrative (**2024-03-21**) points to a supportive environment for financial stocks, potentially aiding revenue generation across segments. GS's own optimistic equity strategy (**2024-02-19**) reflects this.
*   **Strategic Re-focusing:** Actions to divest consumer-facing businesses (GreenSky, personal loans, GM card transition) as noted in the **2024-02-23** 10-K represent a strategic pivot back towards core institutional strengths, which may improve focus and capital efficiency.

### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.**

The franchise remains a dominant global investment bank with a clear strategy to refocus on core institutional services. The near-term macroeconomic backdrop appears favorable, as noted in market and GS's own analysis. However, the qualitative outlook is tempered by ongoing material risks from legacy litigation and recent regulatory actions, which highlight persistent compliance and reputational vulnerabilities. The strategic divestments in Platform Solutions are a positive directional move but their earnings impact is yet to be fully evaluated. The balance of a strong core franchise against significant historical and legal baggage supports a neutral qualitative stance.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'Net Margin: 18.4% as of 2024-03-28, within its 365-day range (0.2–0.2)' — the range notation (0.2–0.2) appears to be a data artifact or formatting error. The sparse history shows net_margin ranging from 0.172 to 0.238 over the window, not 0.2–0.2. The report correctly notes 18.4% but the parenthetical range is misleading.
corrections.required_action: Flag that the net_margin range in the valuation section should read approximately 17.2%–23.8% (0.172–0.238) rather than (0.2–0.2). This is a data presentation error, not a directional issue.

corrections.issue: Quant report states 'ROE: 7.3% as of 2024-03-28, down from 9.6% in April 2023' and the range is given as 'roe 0.1..0.1'. The sparse history shows ROE ranging from 0.067 to 0.096 (6.7%–9.6%), not 0.1–0.1. The range notation is again a data artifact.
corrections.required_action: Flag that the ROE range should read approximately 6.7%–9.6% (0.067–0.096) rather than (0.1–0.1). The directional conclusion about declining ROE is correct and supported by the data.

corrections.issue: Qual report cites '2024-02-22: Goldman Sachs received a disciplinary notice and penalty from ICE's Business Conduct Committee for alleged trading violations dating from July 2022.' The raw evidence confirms this event but provides no detail on the magnitude of the penalty. The qual report characterizes this as a 'material qualitative risk' without quantifying it, which is acceptable given the evidence boundary, but the PM should note the penalty size is not stated in supplied evidence.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed to the report itself, but note for PM: the ICE penalty amount is not stated in supplied evidence, so materiality cannot be independently assessed from the packs.

unsupported_claims: : Quant report's net_margin range '(0.2–0.2)' and ROE range '(0.1..0.1)' are inconsistent with the supplied sparse valuation history showing net_margin of 0.172–0.238 and ROE of 0.067–0.096. These appear to be data formatting artifacts rather than analyst errors, but the displayed ranges are misleading.

: Quant report states 'Price appreciation (+31.2% annual) has outpaced earnings growth' — the report itself shows TTM earnings growth of 51.4% YoY in Q4 2023, which exceeds the 31.2% annual price return. This internal contradiction weakens the 'outpaced' claim. The P/E expansion is better explained by the recovery from depressed 2022-2023 earnings levels rather than price outpacing earnings.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Goldman Sachs trades at cycle-high valuations (P/E 18.3x, P/B 1.24x) with declining ROE (7.3% vs 9.6% a year ago) and volatile earnings, creating an unfavorable risk/reward. The strong Q4 2023 earnings recovery (+63.6% YoY) and favorable macro backdrop provide support, but the stock's +31.2% annual return has already priced in much of the improvement, leaving limited upside at current multiples.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Goldman Sachs trades at cycle-high valuations (P/E 18.3x, P/B 1.24x) with declining ROE (7.3% vs 9.6% a year ago) and volatile earnings, creating an unfavorable risk/reward. The strong Q4 2023 earnings recovery (+63.6% YoY) and favorable macro backdrop provide support, but the stock's +31.2% annual return has already priced in much of the improvement, leaving limited upside at current multiples.
key_factors: Valuation stretched at cycle highs: P/E of 18.3x is at the top of its 365-day range (10.4–18.3) and P/B of 1.24x exceeds its 365-day range (0.9–1.2), leaving little margin of safety after the +31.2% annual price appreciation.

Declining profitability: ROE has fallen from 9.6% (April 2023) to 7.3% (March 2024), and quarterly earnings remain highly volatile (EPS range $3.08–$10.76 over 8 quarters), reflecting sensitivity to capital market conditions.

Earnings recovery priced in: Q4 2023 EPS of $5.48 (+63.6% YoY) and TTM net income of $8.52B represent a recovery from depressed 2022-2023 levels, but the stock's P/E expansion to 18.3x suggests the market has already discounted this improvement.

Ongoing legacy and regulatory risks: A new lawsuit filed 2024-03-17 (ACA Financial Guaranty) alleging CDO fraud and a 2024-02-22 ICE disciplinary penalty for trading violations highlight persistent compliance and reputational vulnerabilities that could generate financial or strategic overhang.

# MCD Quantitative Report — Decision Date 2024-04-01

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow & Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue:** TTM revenue of $25.49B. Quarterly revenue grew YoY from $5.93B (Q4 2022) to $6.41B (Q4 2023), a +8.1% gain. However, QoQ revenue declined −4.3% from $6.69B (Q3 2023), indicating a sequential deceleration after the Q3 peak. The eight-quarter trajectory shows a steady climb from $5.67B (Q1 2022) to $6.69B (Q3 2023), with Q4 2023 pulling back.

**Earnings:** TTM net income of $8.47B. Q4 2023 EPS of $2.80 rose +8.5% YoY from $2.58 but fell −11.7% QoQ from $3.17. The Q2–Q3 2023 period was the earnings peak ($3.15–$3.17 EPS), with Q4 retreating. YoY growth remains positive but the sequential trend is negative.

**Margins:** Operating margin in Q4 2023 was 43.7% ($2.80B / $6.41B), down from 48.0% in Q3 2023 ($3.21B / $6.69B). The latest reported net margin is 33.2% and operating margin is 45.7% (trailing basis). Gross margin across reported quarters is consistently ~90%+ (e.g., Q3 2023: $6.07B / $6.69B = 90.7%).

**Cash Flow:** No cash-flow data is supplied. Free cash flow yield is unavailable.

**Balance Sheet:** Total assets of $56.15B, total liabilities of $60.85B, total equity of **−$4.71B**. Total debt of $39.30B, up from $35.90B in Q4 2022 (+$3.4B or +9.5% YoY). Equity has been persistently negative throughout the period, though improving: from −$6.00B (Q4 2022) to −$4.71B (Q4 2023), a $1.29B improvement. Note: Q2 2023 and Q1 2023 balance-sheet liability figures ($5.00B and $5.78B) appear inconsistent with the accounting identity and are likely truncated in the source data.

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings Quality:** Net margin is stable at ~33% across the trailing four quarters. Operating leverage is strong (45.7% operating margin). However, the absence of cash-flow data prevents verification of earnings-to-cash conversion. The Q4 2023 EPS decline of −11.7% QoQ alongside a −4.3% revenue decline suggests some operating deleverage or cost pressure in the latest quarter.

**Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The negative equity of −$4.71B is a structural feature, reflecting cumulative share repurchases and leverage. Debt of $39.30B against total assets of $56.15B implies a debt-to-assets ratio of 69.9%. The debt increased $3.4B YoY. Without interest coverage or cash-flow data, the debt-servicing capacity cannot be directly assessed from the supplied evidence. The improving equity trend (−$6.00B → −$4.71B) is modestly positive but the absolute leverage level is elevated.

## 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History

**P/E Diluted:** 24.39x as of 2024-03-28. The 12-month range is 22.6x–35.8x. The P/E has compressed significantly from 33.87x (April 2023) to 24.39x, a **−28% multiple contraction** over ~12 months. The current multiple sits near the low end of its one-year range.

**Earnings Yield:** 4.1%, implying the stock is priced to generate roughly a 4% earnings return.

**Net Margin:** Stable at 33.2%, essentially flat versus the 33.3% seen in late 2023. Margin has improved from 26.6% (April 2023) to 33.2%, a meaningful expansion.

**P/S, P/B, EV/EBIT, FCF Yield:** All unavailable or reported as zero (P/S = 0 appears erroneous; a rough implied P/S based on TTM revenue of $25.49B and an estimated market cap of ~$206B would be ~8.1x, but this is not supplied).

**Interpretation:** The P/E compression from ~34x to ~24x has occurred despite stable-to-improving margins and positive YoY earnings growth. The multiple is at the low end of its recent range, but valuation must be assessed conditional on earnings durability — the QoQ earnings deceleration and negative equity/leverage profile are relevant constraints.

## 4. Price Level & Return Context

**Price:** $281.95 (2024-03-28). 52-week range: $245.73–$302.39. The stock sits 6.8% below its 52-week high and 14.7% above its 52-week low.

**Quarterly Return:** −4.3% (from $294.55 on 2023-12-27 to $281.95). This coincides with the QoQ earnings decline of −11.7%.

**Annual Return:** +2.2% (from $275.85 on 2023-03-28 to $281.95). This is a modest gain over 12 months despite +8.5% YoY EPS growth and margin expansion, reflecting the P/E compression offsetting fundamental improvement.

**Quarterly Path:** The stock has been volatile: +5.8% (Q1 2023), −9.6% (Q2 2023), +11.7% (Q3 2023), −4.3% (Q4 2023). The net result over four quarters is roughly flat, while fundamentals (revenue +8.1% YoY, EPS +8.5% YoY) advanced meaningfully — the gap is explained entirely by multiple compression.

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside:**
- P/E reversion: If the P/E returns to the midpoint of its 12-month range (~29x) on current TTM EPS of ~$11.57, the implied price would be ~$335, representing ~19% upside from $281.95.
- Margin expansion: Net margin improved from 26.6% to 33.2% over the past year; further expansion would support earnings growth independent of revenue.
- Equity improvement: The $1.29B YoY improvement in negative equity (−$6.00B → −$4.71B) suggests ongoing deleveraging.

**Downside:**
- Leverage risk: Debt of $39.30B with negative equity of −$4.71B and no cash-flow data leaves debt-servicing capacity unverifiable. A further $3.4B debt increase YoY is a negative trend.
- Earnings deceleration: Q4 2023 EPS of $2.80 is the lowest since Q1 2023 ($2.45) and down −11.7% QoQ. If the QoQ decline persists, the P/E could compress further toward the 22.6x floor, implying a price near ~$262 (−7%).
- Multiple compression risk: The P/E has already compressed 28% in 12 months; further compression is possible if growth disappoints.

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

MCD presents a mixed quantitative picture. On the positive side, YoY fundamentals are solid: revenue +8.1%, EPS +8.5%, net margin expanded from ~27% to ~33%, and the P/E at 24.4x sits near the bottom of its 22.6x–35.8x range. The stock has underperformed its own fundamental improvement, with only +2.2% annual price return versus +8.5% EPS growth.

On the negative side, the balance sheet carries $39.3B in debt against negative $4.7B equity, cash-flow data is entirely absent (preventing quality verification), and the most recent quarter showed meaningful QoQ deterioration in both revenue (−4.3%) and earnings (−11.7%). The P/E compression may reflect rational repricing of durability concerns rather than a simple mean-reversion opportunity.

**Net assessment:** The valuation discount versus recent history is real (24.4x vs. 34x a year ago), but it is conditional on unverifiable cash generation and a structurally leveraged balance sheet. The risk/reward is moderately asymmetric to the upside if Q4 2023's sequential weakness proves seasonal rather than structural, but the absence of cash-flow evidence and the negative equity position are material quantitative constraints that prevent a high-conviction call.
MCD.qualitative_report:
# McDonald's Corporation (MCD) — Qualitative Report
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

---

## 1. Business / Franchise Description

McDonald's is a global quick-service restaurant franchise operator with approximately 41,200 locations worldwide, pursuing an aggressive expansion plan to reach 50,000 locations by 2027 (including the new CosMc's concept) (FranchiseWire, 2024-02-14). The business is asset-light and franchise-dominated, generating $9.61 billion in cash flow from operations in 2023 (Medium, 2024-03-29). The company is characterized as a wide-moat, low-volatility, dividend-paying equity that has regularly boosted dividends across multiple decades (2024-03-28). Technology-enabled delivery is a noted growth lever, with delivery check sizes reportedly 1.5–2× in-store checks (Forbes, 2024-03-04). Multiple analysts describe the franchise's growth catalysts as "robust" (TipRanks, 2024-03-14).

---

## 2. Recent Developments (Dated)

| Date | Development |
|---|---|
| 2024-03-26 | **Partnership:** Krispy Kreme stock soared on announcement of a partnership with McDonald's (supplying doughnuts at McDonald's locations). |
| 2024-03-27 | **Stock Performance:** MCD rose +1.22% in a single session to $282.02, outperforming the broader market. |
| 2024-03-25 | **Insider Activity:** MCD was flagged among stocks where insiders were selling shares. No specific transaction details or context (pre-planned vs. discretionary) were provided. |
| 2024-03-18 | **Legal (Negative):** The U.S. Supreme Court denied review of McDonald's no-poach agreement lawsuit, meaning the company will face a proposed class action alleging antitrust violations that prevented workers from moving to higher-paying locations. |
| 2024-02-28 | **Legal:** Shareholders proceeded with a derivative suit against McDonald's former Chief People Officer under a Delaware ruling, with potential for the former executive to repay millions. |
| 2024-02-14 | **Legal / Product:** A Florida jury returned an $800K verdict against McDonald's for a child burned by hot chicken nuggets, raising food-safety liability visibility. |
| 2024-02-14 | **Growth Plan:** McDonald's announced target of 50,000 global locations by 2027. |

---

## 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets

**Risks:**
- **Litigation / Regulatory Accumulation:** Multiple active lawsuits across distinct categories — antitrust (no-poach, Supreme Court denial 2024-03-18), product liability (burn injury verdict, 2024-02-14), employment law (breast-milk pumping allegations, 2024-02-20), and governance (shareholder derivative suit vs. former CPO, 2024-02-28). While individually manageable, the breadth signals reputational and legal-cost risk.
- **Food Safety Exposure:** As a global franchisor, violations can lead to fines, lawsuits, and brand damage (Studocu context, 2024-02-20). The Florida burn verdict underscores ongoing product-liability tail risk.
- **Insider Selling:** Noted for 2024-03-25, though no details confirm whether sales were pre-scheduled 10b5-1 plans or discretionary.

**Offsets:**
- **Robust Franchise Economics & Cash Generation:** $9.61B operating cash flow (2023) and asset-light model provide financial resilience (Medium, 2024-03-29).
- **Significant Unit-Growth Runway:** Plan to add ~8,800 locations to reach 50,000 by 2027, representing ~21% store base expansion (FranchiseWire, 2024-02-14).
- **Technology & Delivery Upside:** Higher check sizes on delivery orders and ongoing digital investment as growth drivers (Forbes, 2024-03-04).
- **Defensive / Quality Perception:** MCD appears on wide-moat and low-volatility screens, with multiple sources characterizing it as a long-duration compounder and dividend grower.

---

## 4. Qualitative Assessment

**Assessment: Positive (moderate conviction)**

The franchise remains fundamentally strong — asset-light, cash-generative, with a clear 50,000-store expansion target and technology-enabled growth vectors. The Krispy Kreme partnership (2024-03-26) illustrates continued brand-leverage opportunity. However, the accumulation of diverse litigation matters (antitrust, food safety, employment, governance) represents a material qualitative headwind that bears monitoring, particularly the Supreme Court's denial of the no-poach case review (2024-03-18), which now forces class-action proceedings. Insider selling flagged on 2024-03-25 introduces a minor negative signal but is insufficient on its own to alter the franchise-quality thesis. On balance, the durable competitive position and growth outlook outweigh the litigation noise at this stage.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states '$9.61 billion in cash flow from operations in 2023' citing a Medium article, but the raw quantitative evidence pack contains no cash-flow data for any period (all quarters show 'no data'). The quant report correctly flags this absence. The qual report is importing a figure from an external web source that is not present in the raw evidence packs, violating the evidence boundary.
corrections.required_action: Flag that the $9.61B operating cash flow figure is sourced outside the raw evidence packs. The PM should note this figure is unverified against the supplied data and may be accurate but cannot be confirmed from the provided evidence.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Q4 2023 EPS of $2.80 is the lowest since Q1 2023 ($2.45)' — this is internally inconsistent. If Q1 2023 EPS was $2.45, then Q4 2023 at $2.80 is NOT the lowest since Q1 2023; Q1 2023 itself was lower. The claim should read that Q4 2023 is the lowest since Q2 2022 ($1.60) or simply that it is lower than Q2/Q3 2023 but not the lowest in the series.
corrections.required_action: Correct the statement: Q4 2023 EPS of $2.80 is the lowest since Q1 2023 ($2.45) is factually wrong — $2.45 < $2.80. The intended point (sequential deceleration from Q2/Q3 peak) is valid but the specific claim is contradictory.

corrections.issue: The quant report computes Q4 2023 operating margin as 43.7% ($2.80B / $6.41B). The raw data confirms operating_income=2.80B and revenue=6.41B for Q4 2023, so 43.7% is arithmetically correct. However, the report also states the 'latest reported operating margin is 45.7% (trailing basis)' — this is the valuation-ratio operating_margin=0.457 from the raw pack. pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: MCD offers a compelling valuation setup with P/E at 24.4x near the bottom of its 1-year range (22.6x–35.8x) and solid YoY fundamentals (+8.1% revenue, +8.5% EPS growth, net margin expanded to 33.2%), but the structurally impaired balance sheet (negative equity of -$4.71B, debt of $39.3B), absence of verifiable cash-flow data, and Q4 2023 sequential deceleration (-11.7% QoQ EPS) prevent a high-conviction bullish call.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: MCD offers a compelling valuation setup with P/E at 24.4x near the bottom of its 1-year range (22.6x–35.8x) and solid YoY fundamentals (+8.1% revenue, +8.5% EPS growth, net margin expanded to 33.2%), but the structurally impaired balance sheet (negative equity of -$4.71B, debt of $39.3B), absence of verifiable cash-flow data, and Q4 2023 sequential deceleration (-11.7% QoQ EPS) prevent a high-conviction bullish call.
key_factors: P/E of 24.39x sits near the low end of its 12-month range (22.6x–35.8x) after 28% multiple compression from 33.87x a year ago, despite TTM net margin expanding from 26.6% to 33.2% and YoY EPS growth of +8.5% — suggesting potential reversion upside of ~19% if the P/E returns to the ~29x midpoint.

Q4 2023 showed meaningful sequential deceleration: EPS fell -11.7% QoQ to $2.80 (from $3.17 in Q3) and revenue declined -4.3% QoQ to $6.41B, raising questions about whether the Q2-Q3 2023 peak is sustainable or if growth is normalizing.

Balance sheet carries $39.3B in total debt against negative equity of -$4.71B (improving from -$6.00B YoY but still structurally impaired), with debt-to-assets of 69.9% and no cash-flow data available to verify debt-servicing capacity.

Qualitative franchise strength is supported by the 50,000-store expansion target by 2027 (~21% growth), the Krispy Kreme partnership (2024-03-26), and delivery check sizes 1.5-2x in-store levels, though accumulating litigation across antitrust, product liability, and governance categories introduces reputational and legal-cost headwinds.

MRK.quantitative_report:
**MRK Quantitative Report as of 2024-04-01**

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
- Revenue: Q4 2023 revenue of 14.63B, QoQ decline of 8.3% from 15.96B, YoY increase of 5.8% from 13.83B. TTM revenue 60.11B.  
- Earnings: Q4 2023 EPS diluted of -0.48, QoQ down 125.8% from 1.86, YoY down 140.7% from 1.18. TTM net income 377.00M.  
- Margins: Q4 2023 gross margin 73.2% (gross profit 10.72B on 14.63B revenue), operating margin -14.0% (operating income -2.05B). TTM operating margin 3.1% (operating income 1.89B on 60.11B revenue). Net margin TTM 0.6%.  
- Cash-flow: All quarterly cash flow data unavailable.  
- Balance-sheet: Q4 2023 total assets 106.67B, total equity 37.63B (down from 46.06B in Q4 2022), total liabilities 69.04B.

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- Earnings quality: Volatile with negative EPS in Q4 2023 (-0.48) and Q2 2023 (-2.35). TTM net income of 377M on 60.11B revenue yields low net margin of 0.6%. High gross margin of 73.2% offset by operating losses.  
- Balance-sheet resilience: Equity-to-assets ratio of 35.3% (37.63B/106.67B). Equity declined 18.3% from Q4 2022 to Q4 2023. Liabilities of 69.04B exceed equity.

**3. Valuation versus the Company's Own Supplied History**  
- Valuation as of 2024-03-28: PE diluted 942.5, PB 8.93, PS 5.591, earnings yield 0.001.  
- Historical ranges over 365 days: PE diluted 19.1..942.5 (current at high end), PB 5.7..8.9 (current at high end). Net margin and ROE deteriorated from 0.245 and 0.315 in April 2023 to 0.006 and 0.01 in March 2024.

**4. Price Level and Quarterly/Annual Return Context versus Fundamental Path**  
- Price: Latest close 131.95 on 2024-03-28, near 365-day high of 133.10.  
- Returns: Quarterly return (since 2023-12-27) of +22.2%, annual return (since 2023-03-28) of +25.0%.  
- Fundamental path: Earnings deteriorated with recent negative quarters, while price rose significantly.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- Upside: High gross margin of 73.2% and YoY revenue growth of 5.8% support potential earnings recovery.  
- Downside: Negative and volatile earnings, declining equity (37.63B from 46.06B), stretched valuations (PE 942.5, PB 8.93), and unavailable cash flow data.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
- Price momentum (+22.2% quarterly) is decoupled from weak fundamentals (negative TTM net income, low margins). Valuation is elevated relative to history and current earnings (PE 942.5 vs. historical range 19.1..942.5). Quantitative risk of correction if earnings do not improve; reward contingent on earnings normalization, unsupported by current data.
MRK.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: MRK**
**Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Merck is a major pharmaceutical company. Its franchise is centered on Keytruda, a leading immuno-oncology drug, though the supplied text does not detail business segments. The company is actively expanding its portfolio in therapeutic areas like pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) and operates in a highly competitive and regulated industry.

**2. Relevant Recent Developments (Based on Supplied Text)**
*   **Product Catalyst (Positive):** On 2024-03-27, the FDA approved Merck's Winrevair (sotatercept) for PAH. This is labeled the "most important catalyst for Merck's stock in 2024" by Cantor Fitzgerald (2024-03-27). The approval is for a novel mechanism, and analysts noted the label was better than anticipated.
*   **Competitive Threat (Risk):** On 2024-03-26, Stifel initiated coverage on Summit Therapeutics, noting its candidate ivonescimab shows promising potential as a challenger to Merck's Keytruda in lung cancer, with key trial readouts expected in 2025.
*   **Corporate Outlook:** A 2024-03-08 summary of an earnings call indicates Merck forecasts a return to organic growth in 2024, with net sales and EBITDA pre expected to increase slightly to moderately, though the tailwind from COVID-related sales is fading.
*   **Legal/Regulatory (Mixed):** On 2024-03-21, a federal judge dismissed defect claims related to failure-to-warn in HPV vaccine litigation. Separately, Merck is involved in multidistrict litigation regarding Januvia/Janumet (noted 2024-03-11). Pre-dating the specified window, a previously reported cyber insurance dispute was settled in February 2024.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Keytruda Concentration & Competition.** The long-term franchise is heavily reliant on Keytruda. The identified potential challenge from ivonescimab in lung cancer represents a material patent cliff risk if trials succeed (2024-03-26).
*   **Risk: Ongoing Legal Matters.** The company is engaged in significant litigation, including multidistrict litigation for its diabetes drugs (2024-03-11) and the recently dismissed HPV vaccine case (2024-03-21). These create residual financial and reputational risk.
*   **Offset: New Growth Driver.** The FDA approval of Winrevair (2024-03-27) provides a near-term, non-pandemic organic growth catalyst, diversifying revenue streams and potentially mitigating Keytruda dependency risk over time.
*   **Offset: Management Growth Outlook.** The company's own guidance from its 2024-03-08 earnings call points to a return to organic growth, suggesting confidence in its pipeline and commercial execution to offset pandemic revenue declines.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Cautiously Positive.** The adoption of a new, potentially important growth asset (Winrevair) and management's guidance for a return to organic growth are constructive. These are partially offset by significant, ongoing legal proceedings and a credible, though early-stage, competitive threat to its key oncology franchise. The balance of near-term catalysts against longer-term risks results in a cautiously positive qualitative stance.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states Winrevair is for 'pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH)' and the raw evidence confirms FDA approval for PAH. However, the qualitative report does not mention that the raw evidence also references J&J receiving FDA approval for a PAH drug in the same news cycle (2024-03-28 Pharma Stock Roundup). This competitive context is omitted but is present in the supplied packs and could affect the magnitude of the Winrevair catalyst.
corrections.required_action: Note that Winrevair faces immediate competition from J&J's newly approved PAH drug, which may temper the standalone growth catalyst narrative.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report states 'Equity declined 18.3% from Q4 2022 to Q4 2023' (46.06B to 37.63B). The raw balance sheet data shows significant volatility in total_liabilities across quarters — notably negative liability figures in Q2 2023 (-38.74B), Q1 2023 (-46.91B), Q2 2022 (-43.32B), and Q1 2022 (-40.95B). The quant report does not flag or explain these anomalous negative liability entries, which likely reflect data artifacts or reclassifications. This omission could mislead on balance-sheet resilience.
corrections.required_action: Flag the anomalous negative liability figures in the raw balance sheet data as potential data quality issues; the equity-to-assets ratio and liability comparisons should be interpreted with caution.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report's 'Cautiously Positive' stance is reasonable given the Winrevair approval and growth guidance, but it does not weigh the extremely stretched valuation (PE 942.5, PB 8.93 at historical highs) documented in the quantitative report. The qualitative assessment's constructive tone could be read as supporting current price levels without acknowledging the valuation disconnect from fundamentals.
corrections.required_action: The PM should note that the qualitative catalysts (Winrevair, growth guidance) are already reflected in a stock trading at historical valuation extremes with deteriorating earnings, limiting upside from here.

The raw balance sheet data shows significant volatility in total_liabilities across quarters — notably negative liability figures in Q2 2023 (-38.74B), Q1 2023 (-46.91B), Q2 2022 (-43.32B), and Q1 2022 (-40.95B). The quant report does not flag or explain these anomalous negative liability entries, which likely reflect data artifacts or reclassifications. This omission could mislead on balance-sheet resilience. → Flag the anomalous negative liability figures in the raw balance sheet data as potential data quality issues; the equity-to-assets ratio and liability comparisons should be interpreted with caution.
- [C3] The qualitative report's 'Cautiously Positive' stance is reasonable given the Winrevair approval and growth guidance, but it does not weigh the extremely stretched valuation (PE 942.5, PB 8.93 at historical highs) documented in the quantitative report. The qualitative assessment's constructive tone could be read as supporting current price levels without acknowledging the valuation disconnect from fundamentals. → The PM should note that the qualitative catalysts (Winrevair, growth guidance) are already reflected in a stock trading at historical valuation extremes with deteriorating earnings, limiting upside from here.

**Unsupported claims:**
- : The qualitative report states 'the supplied text does not detail business segments' — this is accurate and consistent with the raw evidence (no 10-K business section provided). No unsupported claims found in the quantitative report; all figures match the raw packs.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: MRK presents a classic tension between a genuinely important near-term catalyst (Winrevair approval) and a stock that has already priced in substantial recovery at extreme valuations (PE 942.5, near 365-day highs). The risk/reward is roughly balanced: the new PAH drug and management's growth guidance provide real upside optionality, but deteriorating TTM earnings, declining equity, and competition from J&J's own PAH drug limit the magnitude of further re-rating from here.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: MRK presents a classic tension between a genuinely important near-term catalyst (Winrevair approval) and a stock that has already priced in substantial recovery at extreme valuations (PE 942.5, near 365-day highs). The risk/reward is roughly balanced: the new PAH drug and management's growth guidance provide real upside optionality, but deteriorating TTM earnings, declining equity, and competition from J&J's own PAH drug limit the magnitude of further re-rating from here.
key_factors: Winrevair FDA approval (2024-03-27) is labeled the 'most important catalyst for Merck's stock in 2024' by Cantor Fitzgerald, but faces immediate competition from J&J's newly approved PAH drug (2024-03-28), tempering the standalone growth narrative.

Valuation at historical extremes: PE of 942.5 and PB of 8.93 are at the top of their 365-day ranges (PE 19.1..942.5, PB 5.7..8.9), with the stock near its 365-day high of 133.10, pricing in substantial earnings recovery that TTM data does not yet support.

Earnings trajectory is deteriorating: Q4 2023 EPS of -0.48 (down 125.8% QoQ), TTM net income of only 377M on 60.11B revenue (0.6% net margin), and equity declined 18.3% YoY to 37.63B, while price returned +22.2% quarterly.

Keytruda concentration risk persists with ivonescimab (Summit Therapeutics) showing potential as a lung cancer challenger, with key trial readouts expected in 2025 (Stifel initiation, 2024-03-26), representing a medium-term franchise risk.

Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-ro
…[truncated 9031 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4

are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.

# BA — Quantitative Report (Decision Date: 2024-04-01)

## 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** shows a clear upward trajectory. TTM revenue stands at $77.79B. The latest quarter (Q4 2023) posted $22.02B, the highest in the eight-quarter sample, up +10.2% YoY from Q4 2022's $19.98B and +21.6% QoQ from Q3 2023's $18.10B.

**Earnings** remain firmly negative. Q4 2023 EPS diluted was -$0.03 (net income -$30M), a dramatic improvement versus Q3 2023's -$2.70 and Q2 2023's -$0.25, but TTM net income is -$2.24B. Only one quarter in the eight-quarter window showed positive EPS: Q2 2022 at $0.32. YoY, EPS deteriorated by -97.2% (a misleading percentage given the near-zero denominator in Q4 2023 vs. -$1.06 a year prior).

**Margins** are improving from deeply negative territory. Q4 2023 gross profit was $2.70B on $22.02B revenue (~12.2% gross margin), up from Q3 2022's negative gross profit of -$822M. Operating income turned positive at $283M in Q4 2023, the only positive operating quarter besides Q2 2022 ($774M). TTM operating income is -$773M. Net margin has improved from -7.6% (2023-04-03) to -2.9% (2024-03-28) per the valuation-ratio sparse history.

**Cash-flow** data is entirely unavailable across all reported quarters — no operating, investing, or free cash flow figures are provided.

**Balance sheet** is structurally impaired. Total equity is -$17.23B (Q4 2023), meaning liabilities ($154.24B) exceed assets ($137.01B). Equity has been negative throughout the entire sample, ranging from -$14.79B (Q2 2022) to -$17.64B (Q3 2022). Total debt stands at $52.05B in Q4 2023, down modestly from $57.56B in Q1 2022. Cash on hand was last reported at $14.30B (Q3 2022) — more recent cash balances are unavailable.

---

## 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality:** The near-breakeven Q4 2023 net result (-$30M) was driven by a single quarter of positive operating income ($283M) against a TTM operating loss of -$773M. The swing from Q3 2023's -$808M operating loss to Q4 2023's +$283M operating income is a $1.09B quarterly improvement, raising the question of sustainability absent cash-flow corroboration. Gross profit improved +131.5% QoQ and +45.3% YoY, but the TTM gross margin of 9.9% ($7.72B / $77.79B) and TTM net margin of -2.9% confirm the business is still loss-making on a trailing basis. Seven of the last eight quarters produced negative net income.

**Balance-sheet resilience:** Negative equity of -$17.23B against $52.05B in debt is a significant structural concern. The debt-to-asset ratio is 38.0% ($52.05B / $137.01B), but since equity is negative, the company's net worth is deeply impaired. Without current cash-flow data, debt service capacity cannot be directly verified from the supplied numbers. The most recent cash figure available is $14.30B from Q3 2022 — a material data gap for assessing near-term liquidity.

---

## 3. Valuation Versus Company's Own History

| Metric | Latest (2024-03-28) | Comment |
|---|---|---|
| P/S | 1.503 | Valued on revenue alone given negative earnings |
| P/E | n/a | Negative earnings render P/E undefined |
| EV/EBIT | n/a | Negative TTM operating income |
| FCF yield | n/a | No cash-flow data |
| P/B | n/a | Negative book equity (-$17.23B) |
| Net margin | -2.9% | Improved from -7.6% a year prior |

With no positive earnings, no book equity, and no cash-flow data, traditional valuation anchors are absent. The P/S of 1.503 is the only available multiple. The net margin has improved steadily from -7.6% to -2.9% over the trailing twelve months per the sparse history, but the company remains unprofitable, making any valuation inherently speculative on future margin recovery.

---

## 4. Price Level and Return Context Versus Fundamentals

| 52-week high | $267.54 | -27.9% from high |
| 52-week low | $176.25 | +9.5% from low |

**Annual return:** -5.8% (from $204.96 to $192.99). The stock has underperformed while revenue grew +10.2% YoY and margins improved. The sharp -26.4% quarterly decline (Q1 2024) coincides with a period where the latest earnings showed near-breakeven but the broader balance-sheet and operational uncertainties persist. Price has given back the entire Q3 2023 rally of +34.1%, and current price sits just 9.5% above the 52-week low of $176.25.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside:**
- Revenue growth is accelerating: Q4 2023 at $22.02B is +21.6% QoQ and +10.2% YoY, the strongest quarter in the dataset.
- Operating income turned positive ($283M) after three consecutive negative quarters, suggesting a potential inflection.
- Net margin has improved from -7.6% to -2.9% over the trailing twelve months; if the Q4 2023 run-rate holds (near-zero net loss), profitability could return.
- Debt has declined from $57.56B (Q1 2022) to $52.05B, a $5.5B reduction, indicating some deleveraging.

**Downside:**
- Negative equity of -$17.23B is structural and has not meaningfully improved over eight quarters (range: -$14.79B to -$17.64B).
- TTM operating loss of -$773M and net loss of -$2.24B confirm the business is still destroying capital.
- Complete absence of cash-flow data means there is no confirmation that revenue growth translates into cash generation.
- At $192.99, the P/S of 1.503 prices in recovery expectations for a company with negative book value, $52B in debt, and no current profitability — any operational stumbles would expose significant downside to the 52-week low of $176.25 (-8.7%) or below.
- The Q4 2023 near-breakeven follows a pattern of sharp quarterly swings (Q2 2022 profitable, Q3 2022 deeply unprofitable), providing low confidence in earnings durability.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

BA presents a **negative-skew risk/reward** profile from the numbers alone. The bull case rests on a single quarter of positive operating income ($283M) and improving revenue ($22.02B), but is undermined by: (1) TTM losses of -$2.24B net and -$773M operating, (2) negative equity of -$17.23B with $52.05B in debt, (3) no cash-flow evidence to validate earnings trajectory, and (4) a history of sharp earnings reversals. The stock is priced at 1.5x sales on the assumption of a profitability recovery that has not yet been sustained beyond one quarter. With the price down -26.4% in the latest quarter and sitting only 9.5% above the 52-week low, downside risk to the $176.25 level is quantifiable at -8.7%, while the path to sustained profitability and balance-sheet repair lacks quantitative confirmation in the available data. The burden of proof remains on earnings durability and cash generation, neither of which the supplied evidence supports.
BA.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment: Boeing (BA)**
**Decision Date: 2024-04-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Boeing is a major global aerospace and defense contractor organized into four segments: Commercial Airplanes (BCA), Defense, Space & Security (BDS), Global Services (BGS), and Boeing Capital (BCC). BCA is a leading producer of commercial jetliners (737, 767, 777, 787 families). BDS develops military aircraft, weapons systems, and space products. BGS provides a full spectrum of aftermarket services. The business is heavily regulated, particularly by the FAA, and is highly competitive, facing rivals like Airbus in commercial aviation and Lockheed Martin in defense. Boeing is dependent on a global supply chain for materials and components. (Source: 10-K, filing_date=2023-01-27)

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Management Overhaul (2024-03-26):** CEO David Calhoun and Chairman Larry Kellner announced their decision to step down, a move linked to the investigation into the January 5, 2024, door panel blowout incident on a 737 MAX 9. (Source: Company news, 2024-03-26)
*   **Regulatory Scrutiny (2024-03-21):** The FAA announced a comprehensive audit of Boeing's 737 MAX 9 production line and its suppliers to assess adherence to quality procedures. (Source: Web context [risk], 2024-03-21)
*   **New Contract Win (2024-03-27):** Boeing secured a $96.2 million contract for the MH-47G helicopter program. (Source: Company news, 2024-03-27)
*   **Analyst Sentiment (2024-03-28):** Citi analyst expressed confidence in Boeing's recovery, citing a 31% upside to a $262 price target despite quality control issues. (Source: Company news, 2024-03-28)
*   **Record Backlog (2024-02-13):** Boeing's 2023 Q4 report highlighted a record backlog of $520 billion. (Source: Web context [opportunity], 2024-02-13)
*   **Legal Actions (2024-03-20):** A timeline of Boeing's 2024 troubles noted shareholder lawsuits and allegations from a former Spirit AeroSystems inspector about defects and obscured quality data. (Source: Web context [risk], 2024-03-20)

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Operational & Safety Crisis:** The January 2024 door plug incident has triggered intense FAA scrutiny, production audits, and a leadership crisis, indicating potential systemic quality control failures. (Sources: 2024-03-26, 2024-03-21, 2024-03-20)
    *   **Legal & Financial Liability:** The company faces multiple shareholder and passenger lawsuits related to the incident and prior 737 MAX crashes, creating financial and reputational risk. (Sources: 2024-02-22, 2024-03-04)
    *   **Production & Delivery Disruption:** Regulatory audits and mandated corrective actions could further slow 737 MAX deliveries, impacting revenue and cash flow. (Inference from 2024-03-21)
    *   **Competitive Pressure:** Airbus and emerging competitors (e.g., China's Comac) continue to challenge Boeing's market position. (Source: 10-K, 2023-01-27; Web context [opportunity], 2024-03-26)
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Strong Commercial Backlog:** A record $520 billion backlog provides long-term revenue visibility, assuming production can stabilize. (Source: 2024-02-13)
    *   **Diversified Defense Portfolio:** Ongoing contract wins (e.g., MH-47G) and a broad defense/space portfolio provide stability outside the commercial segment. (Source: 2024-03-27)
    *   **Management Reset:** The leadership change, while disruptive, is viewed by some analysts as a potential turning point to address cultural and operational issues. (Source: 2024-03-26)

**4. Qualitative Assessment**
**Negative.** The point-in-time evidence leading into April 2024 is dominated by a severe operational and reputational crisis stemming from the January 2024 safety incident. The resulting FAA investigation, management exodus, and legal actions present material, near-term risks to production, financials, and brand equity. While the company's long-term backlog and defense business provide a foundation, the immediate narrative is one of significant regulatory and execution uncertainty that outweighs these structural positives.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states the Citi analyst price target is $262, but the raw evidence headline says 'Boeing Stock Has 31% Upside' and separately '1 Wall Street Analyst Thinks Boeing Stock Is Going to $262.' The 31% upside figure and $262 target are consistent with each other (31% upside from ~$191 ≈ $250, close enough given rounding), but the qual report attributes the $262 target to Citi while the raw evidence headline for the $262 target does not name Citi — the Citi attribution appears in a separate headline. This is a minor sourcing ambiguity, not a material conflict.
corrections.required_action: No action required; the claim is directionally supported by the raw evidence even if the specific Citi-to-$262 linkage is assembled from two separate headlines.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states 'Production & Delivery Disruption' risk is an 'Inference from 2024-03-21' — this is correctly flagged as inference rather than a direct evidence quote, which is good practice. However, the raw evidence from 2024-02-13 (Boeing 2023 Q4 Report) also notes '$1.6 billion in additional losses were booked on fixed-price contracts of Boeing's five most significant development programs,' which is a material financial risk item present in the raw packs but not surfaced in either specialist report.
corrections.required_action: PM should note the $1.6B additional fixed-price contract losses from the Q4 report as a material earnings-quality headwind not fully captured in the specialist analyses.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report correctly identifies the complete absence of cash-flow data across all eight quarters. The balance-sheet section notes cash was last reported at $14.30B (Q3 2022) and flags this as a material data gap. This is an accurate and important caveat. No correction needed, but the PM should be aware that the inability to assess cash generation is a fundamental limitation of this analysis.
corrections.required_action: No correction needed; the data gap is properly flagged.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing's duopoly franchise and $520B record backlog provide a structural floor, but the January 2024 737 MAX 9 door plug incident has triggered a severe operational and regulatory crisis — including CEO departure, FAA production audits, and legal exposure — that makes near-term earnings recovery highly uncertain. With TTM net losses of -$2.24B, negative equity of -$17.23B, and no cash-flow data to validate the Q4 2023 near-breakeven, risk/reward is roughly balanced at $193, sitting 9.5% above the 52-week low.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing's duopoly franchise and $520B record backlog provide a structural floor, but the January 2024 737 MAX 9 door plug incident has triggered a severe operational and regulatory crisis — including CEO departure, FAA production audits, and legal exposure — that makes near-term earnings recovery highly uncertain. With TTM net losses of -$2.24B, negative equity of -$17.23B, and no cash-flow data to validate the Q4 2023 near-breakeven, risk/reward is roughly balanced at $193, sitting 9.5% above the 52-week low.
key_factors: Revenue trajectory is strong: Q4 2023 revenue of $22.02B was the highest in eight quarters, up +10.2% YoY and +21.6% QoQ, with TTM revenue of $77.79B and a record $520B backlog providing long-term visibility.

Balance sheet remains structurally impaired: negative equity of -$17.23B, total debt of $52.05B, TTM net loss of -$2.24B, and no cash-flow data available to confirm revenue is converting to cash generation.

The January 2024 737 MAX 9 door plug incident has created a severe operational crisis: CEO Calhoun announced departure on 2024-03-26, FAA launched comprehensive production audits on 2024-03-21, and $1.6B in additional fixed-price contract losses were booked in Q4 2023.

Q4 2023 showed a potential earnings inflection with positive operating income of $283M and near-breakeven net income (-$30M), but this follows a pattern of sharp quarterly reversals — only one of the prior seven quarters was profitable — providing low confidence in durability.

Embracing Our Future
Microsoft is a technology company whose mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. We strive to create local opportunity, growth, and impact in every country around the world. We are creating the platforms and tools, powered by artificial intelligence ("AI"), that deliver better, faster, and more effective solutions to support small and large business competitiveness, improve educational and health outcomes, grow public-sector efficiency,  and empower human ingenuity. Item 1
As of June 30, 2023, we employed approximately 221,000 people on a full-time basis, 120,000 in the U.S. and 101,000 internationally. Of the total employed people, 89,000 were in operations, including manufacturing, distribution, product support, and consulting services; 72,000 were in product research and development; 45,000 were in sales and marketing; and 15,000 were in general and administration. Certain employees are subject to collective bargaining agreements.
Our Culture
Microsoft's culture is grounded in growth mindset. This means everyone is on a continuous journey to learn and grow, operating as one company instead of multiple siloed businesses.
Our employee listening systems enable us to gather feedback directly from our workforce to inform our programs and employee needs globally. Employees participate in our Employee Signals surveys, which cover a variety of topics such as thriving, inclusion, team culture, wellbeing, and learning and development. We also collect Daily Signals employee survey responses, giving us real-time insights into ways we can support our employees. In addition to Employee Signals and Daily Signals surveys, we gain insights through onboarding, exit surveys, internal Viva Engage channels, employee Q&A sessions, and our internal AskHR Service support.
Diversity and inclusion are core to our business model, and we hold ourselves accountable for driving global systemic change in our workforce and creating an inclusive work environment. We support multiple highly active Employee Resource Groups for women, families, racial and ethnic minorities, military, people with disabilities, and employees who identify as LGBTQIA+, where employees can go for support, networking, and community-building. As described in our 2022 Proxy Statement, annual performance and compensation reviews of our senior leadership team include an evaluation of their contributions to employee culture and diversity. To ensure accountability over time, we publicly disclose our progress on a multitude of workforce metrics including:
- Detailed breakdowns of gender, racial, and ethnic minority representation in our employee population, with data by job types, levels, and segments of our business.
- Our EEO-1 report (equal employment opportunity).
- Disability representation.
- Pay equity (see details below).
Total Rewards and Pay Equity
We develop dynamic, sustainable, market-driven, and strategic programs with the goal of providing a highly differentiated portfolio to attract, reward, and retain top talent and enable our employees to thrive. These programs reinforce our culture and values such as collaboration and growth mindset. Managers evaluate and recommend rewards based on, for example, how well we leverage the work of others and contribute to the success of our colleagues. We monitor pay equity and career progress across multiple dimensions. Our total compensation opportunity is highly differentiated and is market competitive.
In order to manage our costs in a dynamic, competitive environment, in fiscal year 2023 we announced that base salaries of salaried employees would remain at fiscal year 2022 levels. Pay increases continue to be available for rewards-eligible hourly and equivalent employees. We will continue our practice of investing in stock for all rewards-eligible employees, salaried and hourly, and investing in bonuses for all eligible employees.
9
Item 1
Since 2016, we have reported on pay equity as part of our annual Diversity and Inclusion report. In 2022, we reported that all racial and ethnic minority employees in the U.S. combined earn $1.008 for every $1.000 earned by their white counterparts, that women in the U.S. earn $1.007 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts who are men, and that women outside the U.S. earn $1.002 for every $1.000 earned by their counterparts outside the U.S. who are men. In this year's report, we again expanded our pay equity data beyond the U.S. to report on 61 additional countries (up from 12 last year), representing 99.8% of our global Microsoft workforce.
In addition, we began reporting on unadjusted median pay in our annual report, comparing total pay amounts for all employees regardless of factors such as job title, level, or tenure. For employees who are eligible for rewards, the analysis showed that total pay for women is 89.6% of total pay for men in the U.S. and 86.2% outside of the U.S., and total pay for racial and ethnic minorities in the U.S. is 89.9% of total pay for white employees. As we continue to increase representation for women and racial and ethnic minorities at more senior levels, and continue to ensure pay equity for all, the gap between the medians will reduce.
Our intended result is a global performance and development approach that fosters our culture, and competitive compensation that ensures equitable pay by role while supporting pay for performance.
Wellbeing and Hybrid Work
Microsoft is committed to supporting our employees' wellbeing while they are at work and in their personal lives. We have invested significantly in wellbeing, and offer a differentiated benefits package which includes many physical, emotional, and financial wellness programs including counseling through the Microsoft CARES Employee Assistance Program, mental wellbeing support, flexible fitness benefits, disability accommodations, savings and investment tools, adoption assistance, and back-up care for children and elders. Finally, our Occupational Health and Safety program helps ensure employees can stay safe while they are working.
We introduced Hybrid Workplace Flexibility Guidance to better support leaders, managers, and employees in hybrid work scenarios. Our ongoing survey data shows that 93% of employees value the flexibility related to work location, work site, and work hours, and 78% are satisfied with the quality of connection with co-workers. There is no one-size-fits-all approach to flexible work at Microsoft. As a company, we will continue to leverage data and research to inform decision making, balancing the needs of business, team, and individual.
Learning and Development
We offer a range of learning opportunities, including personalized opportunities on our internal and external learning portals, in-classroom learning, required learning on compliance and company culture, on-the-job advancement opportunities, and manager coaching. We also provide customized manager learning, new employee orientation, and tools for operating in a flexible hybrid work environment.
All Microsoft employees globally access our single Viva Learning tool for both required and personal choice learning. This includes courses focused on our core principles and compliance matters, such as Business Conduct, Privacy, Security Foundations, and Harassment Prevention. We also deliver skills training for employees based on their profession and role discipline.
We have over 27,000 people managers, all of whom must complete between 20-33 hours of compulsory training on leadership and management and are assigned additional targeted training on an ongoing basis related to people management, compliance, and culture.
10
Item 1
OPERATING SEGMENTS
We operate our business and report our financial performance using three segments: Productivity and Business Processes, Intelligent Cloud, and More Personal Computing. Our segments provide management with a comprehensive financial view of our key businesses. The segments enable the alignment of strategies and objectives across the development, sales, marketing, and services organizations, and they provide a framework for timely and rational allocation of resources within businesses.
Additional information on our operating segments and geographic and product information is contained in Note 19 – Segment Info
